prompt: Non avevo fame: ____ poco prima. (mangiare, io)
explanation: 'Non avevo fame: avevo mangiato poco prima.' means 'I wasn't hungry: I'd eaten a little earlier.' When you say something had already happened before another past moment, Italian uses the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. You build it just like the passato prossimo but with the auxiliary in the imperfect: avevo, not ho. The imperfect of avere for io is avevo and the participle of mangiare is mangiato, giving avevo mangiato. Writing ho mangiato would be the passato prossimo, a plain past that loses the earlier-than relationship.

prompt: Sapevo già come finiva il film perché lo ____ due volte. (vedere, io)
explanation: 'Sapevo già come finiva il film perché lo avevo visto due volte.' means 'I already knew how the film ended because I'd seen it twice.' The film had been seen before the moment of knowing the ending, so this is the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere plus the participle. For io the imperfect of avere is avevo, and vedere has the irregular participle visto, giving avevo visto. Ho visto would be the passato prossimo and would not place the watching before the knowing.

prompt: All'esame eri tranquillo perché ____ molto. (studiare, tu)
explanation: 'All'esame eri tranquillo perché avevi studiato molto.' means 'You were relaxed at the exam because you'd studied hard.' The studying happened before the calm at the exam, so it takes the trapassato prossimo. Build it from the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for tu the imperfect of avere is avevi and the participle of studiare is studiato, so avevi studiato. Hai studiato is the passato prossimo and drops the before relationship the sentence needs.

prompt: La sera eri stanco perché ____ troppo durante il giorno. (fare, tu)
explanation: 'La sera eri stanco perché avevi fatto troppo durante il giorno.' means 'In the evening you were tired because you'd done too much during the day.' The doing came before the tiredness, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevi for tu, and fare has the irregular participle fatto, giving avevi fatto. Hai fatto would be the passato prossimo, a plain past.

prompt: Marco conosceva già la risposta perché ____ il manuale. (leggere)
explanation: 'Marco conosceva già la risposta perché aveva letto il manuale.' means 'Marco already knew the answer because he'd read the manual.' The reading preceded already knowing the answer, so the trapassato prossimo is wanted. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for lui, and leggere has the irregular participle letto, so aveva letto. Ha letto is the passato prossimo and would not set the reading before the knowing. The subject Marco shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.

prompt: I bambini non avevano fame: la mamma ____ loro la merenda poco prima. (preparare)
explanation: 'I bambini non avevano fame: la mamma aveva preparato loro la merenda poco prima.' means 'The children weren't hungry: their mum had made them a snack a little earlier.' The snack was prepared before the children were not hungry, so this is the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. Take the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for la mamma (lei) and preparato for preparare, giving aveva preparato. Ha preparato would be the passato prossimo and lose the earlier-than sense.

prompt: Non fui sorpreso: Anna mi ____ tutto il giorno prima. (dire)
explanation: 'Non fui sorpreso: Anna mi aveva detto tutto il giorno prima.' means 'I wasn't surprised: Anna had told me everything the day before.' The telling happened the day before the lack of surprise, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for Anna (lei), and dire has the irregular participle detto, so aveva detto. The mi here is an indirect object (to me), so the participle does not change. Ha detto would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Eravamo esausti perché ____ tutto il giorno. (lavorare, noi)
explanation: 'Eravamo esausti perché avevamo lavorato tutto il giorno.' means 'We were exhausted because we'd worked all day.' The working led up to the exhaustion, an action complete before another past point, so the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for noi the imperfect of avere is avevamo and the participle of lavorare is lavorato, giving avevamo lavorato. Abbiamo lavorato is the passato prossimo.

prompt: Arrivammo in orario perché ____ un taxi. (prendere, noi)
explanation: 'Arrivammo in orario perché avevamo preso un taxi.' means 'We arrived on time because we'd taken a taxi.' Taking the taxi came before arriving on time, so the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevamo for noi, and prendere has the irregular participle preso, so avevamo preso. Abbiamo preso would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: L'esame andò bene perché ____ tutto a lezione. (capire, voi)
explanation: 'L'esame andò bene perché avevate capito tutto a lezione.' means 'The exam went well because you'd understood everything in class.' The understanding preceded the exam going well, so the trapassato prossimo. Build it from the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for voi the imperfect of avere is avevate and the participle of capire is capito, giving avevate capito. Avete capito is the passato prossimo, a plain past.

prompt: Il professore era contento perché ____ un buon tema. (scrivere, voi)
explanation: 'Il professore era contento perché avevate scritto un buon tema.' means 'The teacher was pleased because you'd written a good essay.' The essay was written before the professor was pleased, a past-before-past, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevate for voi, and scrivere has the irregular participle scritto, so avevate scritto. Avete scritto would be the passato prossimo.

prompt: I ragazzi uscirono presto perché ____ i compiti nel pomeriggio. (finire)
explanation: 'I ragazzi uscirono presto perché avevano finito i compiti nel pomeriggio.' means 'The boys went out early because they'd finished their homework in the afternoon.' Finishing the homework came before going out, so the action sits in the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: for loro the imperfect of avere is avevano and the participle of finire is finito, giving avevano finito. Hanno finito is the passato prossimo. The subject i ragazzi shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.

prompt: Il negozio era vuoto perché ____ quasi tutto. (vendere, loro)
explanation: 'Il negozio era vuoto perché avevano venduto quasi tutto.' means 'The shop was empty because they'd sold almost everything.' Selling almost everything happened before the shop was empty, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevano for loro, and a regular -ere verb forms its participle in -uto, so vendere gives venduto and the whole form is avevano venduto. Hanno venduto would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Paolo non era preoccupato perché ____ alle parole di Maria. (credere, trapassato prossimo / pluperfect)

must_not_include.note: credeva is the imperfetto (an ongoing state); the cue asks for the trapassato prossimo, the completed prior action: aveva creduto

explanation: 'Paolo non era preoccupato perché aveva creduto alle parole di Maria.' means 'Paolo wasn't worried because he'd believed what Maria said.' Believing Maria's words came before the calm, so this is the past-before-past, the trapassato prossimo (English 'pluperfect'). Take the imperfect of avere plus the participle: aveva for Paolo (lui), and credere is a regular -ere verb whose participle is creduto, giving aveva creduto. Ha creduto would be the passato prossimo; credeva would be the imperfect, an ongoing state rather than the completed prior action the cue asks for.

prompt: La mattina ero riposato perché ____ bene. (dormire, io)
explanation: 'La mattina ero riposato perché avevo dormito bene.' means 'In the morning I felt rested because I'd slept well.' The sleeping came before feeling rested in the morning, so the trapassato prossimo. It is the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevo for io, and a regular -ire verb forms its participle in -ito, so dormire gives dormito and the form is avevo dormito. Ho dormito would be a plain passato prossimo.

prompt: Non andammo al supermercato perché ____ tutto il giorno prima. (comprare, noi)
explanation: 'Non andammo al supermercato perché avevamo comprato tutto il giorno prima.' means 'We didn't go to the supermarket because we'd bought everything the day before.' The buying happened the day before, ahead of the decision not to go shopping, so the trapassato prossimo. Use the imperfect of avere plus the participle: avevamo for noi, and a regular -are verb forms its participle in -ato, so comprare gives comprato and the form is avevamo comprato. Abbiamo comprato is the passato prossimo.

prompt: Cercavo le chiavi dappertutto: le ____ in tasca poco prima! (mettere, io)
explanation: 'Cercavo le chiavi dappertutto: le avevo messe in tasca poco prima!' means 'I was hunting everywhere for the keys: I'd put them in my pocket a little earlier!' The keys had been put in the pocket before the searching, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere (avevo) plus the participle. There is also a preceding direct-object pronoun le (standing for le chiavi), and with a preceding direct-object pronoun the participle agrees, a rule carried over from the passato prossimo. Le chiavi are feminine plural, so the participle of mettere becomes messe, giving le avevo messe. The present auxiliary (ho) would make it a passato prossimo, and messo or messa would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree) and preceding-DOP agreement (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo bucket). The aux markpoint matches the bare auxiliary so the agreement markpoint scores independently.

prompt: Quando siamo arrivati, la torta era già in tavola: Maria l'____ la sera prima. (fare)
explanation: 'Quando siamo arrivati, la torta era già in tavola: Maria l'aveva fatta la sera prima.' means 'When we arrived, the cake was already on the table: Maria had made it the evening before.' The cake had been made before we arrived, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of avere (aveva for Maria) plus the participle. The elided l' is a preceding direct-object pronoun standing for la torta, and with a preceding direct-object pronoun the participle agrees, the passato prossimo rule inherited. La torta is feminine singular, so fare's irregular participle fatto becomes fatta, giving l'aveva fatta. The present auxiliary (ha) would be a passato prossimo, and fatto would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: imperfect auxiliary (this tree) and preceding-DOP agreement (inherited, PP bucket). The aux markpoint matches the bare auxiliary.

prompt: Quando sono arrivato, Marco non c'era più: ____ a casa poco prima. (andare)
explanation: 'Quando sono arrivato, Marco non c'era più: era andato a casa poco prima.' means 'When I arrived, Marco was no longer there: he'd gone home a little earlier.' Marco's going home happened before the arrival, so the trapassato prossimo. With essere verbs it is the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lui the imperfect of essere is era, and andare gives andato, agreeing as masculine singular, so era andato. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a non-agreeing ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Quando ho telefonato, Lucia ____ da poco. (partire)
explanation: 'Quando ho telefonato, Lucia era partita da poco.' means 'When I phoned, Lucia had left a short time before.' Lucia had already left a little before the phone call, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lei the imperfect of essere is era, and partire gives partito, agreeing as feminine singular to partita, so era partita. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and partito would not agree with the feminine subject.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Noi ragazzi eravamo stanchi perché ____ a piedi. (venire)
explanation: 'Noi ragazzi eravamo stanchi perché eravamo venuti a piedi.' means 'We boys were tired because we'd come on foot.' Coming on foot happened before the tiredness, so the trapassato prossimo with essere: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For noi the imperfect of essere is eravamo, and venire gives venuto, agreeing as masculine plural to venuti, so eravamo venuti. Siamo venuti would be a passato prossimo, and a singular ending would miss the agreement. The subject noi ragazzi shows the person, so the cue names only the verb.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Le ragazze non c'erano: ____ poco prima. (uscire)
explanation: 'Le ragazze non c'erano: erano uscite poco prima.' means 'The girls weren't there: they'd gone out a little earlier.' The girls had gone out before they were found missing, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and uscire gives uscito, agreeing as feminine plural to uscite, so erano uscite. Sono uscite would be a passato prossimo, and uscito or usciti would miss the agreement with the feminine plural subject.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Anna, alla festa sembravi riposata: ____ a casa presto la sera prima? (tornare, tu)

translation.reveal: tornare - to return / to go back

explanation: 'Anna, alla festa sembravi riposata: eri tornata a casa presto la sera prima?' means 'Anna, you looked rested at the party: had you got home early the evening before?' Returning home came before the party, so the trapassato prossimo with essere: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For tu the imperfect of essere is eri, and tornare gives tornato, agreeing with the feminine subject Anna to tornata, so eri tornata. Sei tornata would be a passato prossimo, and tornato would not agree.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds. Subject Anna fixes feminine singular agreement.

prompt: Quando vi ho chiamati, ____ a casa da un'ora, vero? (arrivare, voi)
explanation: 'Quando vi ho chiamati, eravate arrivati a casa da un'ora, vero?' means 'When I rang you, you'd got home an hour earlier, hadn't you?' Arriving home had happened an hour before the call, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For voi the imperfect of essere is eravate, and arrivare gives arrivato, agreeing as masculine plural to arrivati, so eravate arrivati. Siete arrivati would be a passato prossimo, and a singular or feminine ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Il nonno raccontava che ____ in un piccolo paese. (nascere)
explanation: 'Il nonno raccontava che era nato in un piccolo paese.' means 'Grandad used to tell us that he'd been born in a small village.' Being born sits before the grandfather's telling, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lui the imperfect of essere is era, and nascere has the irregular participle nato, agreeing as masculine singular, so era nato. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a feminine or plural ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Quando tornai al paese, il vecchio medico ____ da anni. (morire)
explanation: 'Quando tornai al paese, il vecchio medico era morto da anni.' means 'When I went back to the village, the old doctor had been dead for years.' The doctor had died years before the return, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lui the imperfect of essere is era, and morire has the irregular participle morto, agreeing as masculine singular, so era morto. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a feminine or plural ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). prompt: La festa era finita ma alcuni amici ____ ad aiutare. (rimanere)
explanation: 'La festa era finita ma alcuni amici erano rimasti ad aiutare.' means 'The party was over but some friends had stayed behind to help.' Some friends had stayed on before the party was over, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and rimanere has the irregular participle rimasto, agreeing as masculine plural to rimasti, so erano rimasti. Sono rimasti would be a passato prossimo, and a singular ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. prompt: C'era acqua per terra: un bicchiere ____ poco prima. (cadere)
explanation: 'C'era acqua per terra: un bicchiere era caduto poco prima.' means 'There was water on the floor: a glass had fallen a little earlier.' A glass had fallen before the water was on the floor, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For the singular subject un bicchiere the imperfect of essere is era, and cadere gives caduto, agreeing as masculine singular, so era caduto. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a feminine or plural ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Marco e Lucia non risposero: ____ a dormire poco prima. (andare)
explanation: 'Marco e Lucia non risposero: erano andati a dormire poco prima.' means 'Marco and Lucia didn't answer: they'd gone to bed a little earlier.' Marco and Lucia had gone to bed before failing to answer, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and a mixed-gender subject (a man and a woman) takes the masculine plural by default, so andare gives andati and the form is erano andati. Sono andati would be a passato prossimo, and andate would wrongly treat the pair as all feminine.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds. Mixed-gender subject resolves to masculine plural.

prompt: Ho scoperto che le mie sorelle ____ per Roma il giorno prima. (partire)
explanation: 'Ho scoperto che le mie sorelle erano partite per Roma il giorno prima.' means 'I found out that my sisters had left for Rome the day before.' The leaving happened before I found out, so the trapassato prossimo (English 'pluperfect'): the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For loro the imperfect of essere is erano, and partire gives partito, agreeing as feminine plural to partite, so erano partite. Sono partite would be a passato prossimo, and partiti would miss the feminine agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.

prompt: Quando il film cominciò, Giulia ____ al cinema da dieci minuti. (arrivare)
explanation: 'Quando il film cominciò, Giulia era arrivata al cinema da dieci minuti.' means 'When the film started, Giulia had got to the cinema ten minutes earlier.' Giulia had arrived ten minutes before the film began, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lei the imperfect of essere is era, and arrivare gives arrivato, agreeing with the feminine subject to arrivata, so era arrivata. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and arrivato would not agree.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. Paolo had gone out before the knocking, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For lui the imperfect of essere is era, and uscire gives uscito, agreeing as masculine singular, so era uscito. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a feminine or plural ending would miss the agreement.

prompt: Quando il capo entrò, noi ragazze ____ da poco. (arrivare)
explanation: 'Quando il capo entrò, noi ragazze eravamo arrivate da poco.' means 'When the boss came in, we girls had only arrived a short time before.' The arriving sits a little before the boss's entrance, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. For noi the imperfect of essere is eravamo, and arrivare gives arrivato, agreeing as feminine plural to arrivate, so eravamo arrivate. Siamo arrivate would be a passato prossimo, and arrivati would miss the feminine agreement.

prompt: La casa era silenziosa: nessuno ____ da ore. (entrare)

translation.reveal: entrare - to enter / to come in

explanation: 'La casa era silenziosa: nessuno era entrato da ore.' means 'The house was silent: nobody had come in for hours.' No one had entered for hours before the silence, so the trapassato prossimo: the imperfect of essere plus the participle, agreeing with the subject. The subject nessuno is masculine singular, the imperfect of essere is era, and entrare gives entrato, so era entrato. The present auxiliary (è) would be a passato prossimo, and a feminine or plural ending would miss the agreement.
examiner_note: Two skills: the imperfect auxiliary (this tree, the new skill) and participle agreement with essere (inherited, scored against the passato prossimo agreement bucket). The aux markpoint matches the imperfect auxiliary and the agreement markpoint matches the participle ending; they score independently. The aux markpoint tests imperfect-vs-present, not person, which the prompt's subject scaffolds.
